commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a same-session A2A delivery bypass that sends the latest reply directly to the resolved source channel and adds a regression test for Discord channel sessions.

PR surface: Source +34, Tests +22. Total +56 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction was run in this read-only review. The source path is reproducible by forcing the announce step to return ANNOUNCE_SKIP: current main then skips gateway send after sessions_send has accepted/pending delivery.
commands.review_followup: The next action is maintainer boundary review plus contributor or maintainer real-behavior proof; there is no narrow automated code repair from this review.

target.title: fix(discord): deliver same-session channel replies
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Fixes same-session Discord guild-channel sessions_send replies being dropped when the async A2A announce decider returns ANNOUNCE_SKIP.
- Keeps true cross-agent announcement routing on the existing announce-decider path.

Why does this matter now?

- Channel-originated sessions can already produce the correct assistant reply, but users never see it when the decider treats it like an agent-to-agent announcement.

What is the intended outcome?

- When a channel session sends to itself, the latest assistant reply is delivered directly back to the source channel.
- Cross-session and cross-agent announcements still use the existing decider and ping-pong guard behavior.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- No changes to Discord configuration, provider wiring, or broader A2A announcement policy.
- Production Discord guild delivery is still pending reviewer direction before marking the PR ready.

What does success look like?

- A same-session Discord channel reply sends the substantive assistant response instead of returning ANNOUNCE_SKIP.
- Existing cross-agent announce behavior remains unchanged.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether same-session source-channel replies are the right boundary for bypassing the announce decider.
- Whether the helper extraction preserves the existing delivery semantics for non-same-session A2A flows.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #87157

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #86723
Related #86725

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

- Requested by Steven after investigating the Discord guild-channel reply path described in #87157.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Same-session Discord guild-channel replies can be generated but skipped by the A2A announce decider before reaching the source channel.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw runtime from this PR branch using node and the patched sessions_send A2A module.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran a node --import tsx --input-type=module runtime harness that invoked runSessionsSendA2AFlow for agent:main:discord:channel:87157-proof with requesterSessionKey equal to targetSessionKey and roundOneReply set to visible reply for issue 87157.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Console output from the node run:
  {
    "scenario": "same-session Discord channel reply",
    "sendCalls": 1,
    "firstSend": {
      "method": "send",
      "params": {
        "channel": "discord",
        "to": "channel:87157-proof",
        "message": "visible reply for issue 87157",
        "hasIdempotencyKey": true
      },
      "timeoutMs": 10000
    }
  }
- Observed result after fix: The same-session Discord channel flow produced one gateway send call to channel:87157-proof with message visible reply for issue 87157 and an idempotency key, so the reply is delivered directly to the source channel path instead of being converted into ANNOUNCE_SKIP.
- What was not tested: Production Discord guild-channel delivery and the full repository validation suite.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This draft is waiting on CI, ClawSweeper, and maintainer guidance for any required production Discord proof.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): #87157 reports sessions_send returning accepted/pending while logs show ANNOUNCE_SKIP, leaving the Discord channel without the generated reply.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- git diff --check HEAD~1..HEAD
- ./node_modules/.bin/tsx -e "import('./src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.ts').then((mod)=>console.log(typeof mod.runSessionsSendA2AFlow))"
- node --import tsx --input-type=module runtime harness for the same-session Discord channel path
- CI=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --reporter=verbose src/agents/tools/sessions-send-tool.a2a.test.ts (bounded local attempt; did not complete before termination)

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added a regression test covering same-session channel replies bypassing the announce decider and delivering the substantive reply directly.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- In the issue path, the assistant response was generated but the A2A announcement phase returned ANNOUNCE_SKIP, so the Discord channel never received the reply.

If no test was added, why not?

- A focused regression test was added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (Yes/No)

Yes.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (Yes/No)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (Yes/No)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

- Accidentally bypassing the announce-decider for flows that are actually cross-agent announcements.

How is that risk mitigated?

- The bypass is limited to requesterSessionKey == targetSessionKey with an existing announce target, and cross-session flows still use the existing decider path.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

- CI and ClawSweeper are rerunning after the proof body update.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

- Waiting on CI/ClawSweeper.
- Waiting on maintainer direction for whether production Discord guild-channel proof is required before marking ready for review.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

- Addressed the Real behavior proof gate by adding copied console output from a node run against this PR branch.
